backend/main.mo: Main backend actor defining analysis types (direction, patterns, resistance levels), user settings with M1/M5/M10 timeframes, and functions to store/retrieve analyses and settings per user with validation.
backend/migration.mo: Migration module converting old Nat-based timeframe settings to new variant-based Timeframe type (#M1, #M5, #M10) for existing user data.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report documenting Portuguese translation requirement, M1/M5/M10 timeframe restriction, and Results screen updates with timeframe display and directional arrows.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: Pre-existing summaries of all frontend components showing Portuguese translations, M1/M5/M10 timeframe restrictions, and directional arrow symbols already implemented.
frontend/index.css: Tailwind CSS configuration with custom CSS variables for light and dark theme colors including background, foreground, primary, secondary, and chart colors.
frontend/index.html: HTML entry point with root div and module script loading main.tsx, titled 'Advance Signal Pro'.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Root React component setting up TanStack Router with routes for dashboard, analyze, processing, results, history, and settings, wrapped in ThemeProvider.
frontend/src/components/CameraCapture.ts: Camera capture component with Portuguese UI text, using useCamera hook for photo capture with retake functionality, error handling for permissions/support, and mobile camera switching.
frontend/src/components/ChartOverlay.tsx: React component that renders a canvas overlay on top of a chart image, drawing resistance levels as dashed horizontal lines and trend arrows (bullish/bearish) based on analysis data.
frontend/src/components/FileUpload.tsx: File upload component with drag-and-drop support that validates image/PDF files (max 10MB), displays selected file info, and provides clear functionality.
Displays a clickable card for a single analysis result in history, showing direction badge, confidence percentage, timestamp, and trend information; navigates to results page on click.
frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx: Root layout component that wraps the app with a main content area, Toaster for notifications, and a footer with attribution to caffeine.ai.
Displays a single processing stage indicator with three states: inactive (dot), active (spinning loader), or complete (checkmark).
frontend/src/components/SettingRow.tsx: Simple layout component for settings UI that displays a label, description, and control element in a horizontal row.
frontend/src/components/SignalCard.tsx: Displays trading signal analysis results in a card with direction (buy/sell/hold), confidence percentage, trend strength, and appropriate icons and colors.
frontend/src/context/ThemeContext.tsx: React context provider for theme management (light/dark mode) with localStorage persistence and document class manipulation.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: React Query hook that creates and manages an ICP actor instance based on Internet Identity authentication state, invalidating dependent queries when identity changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useClipboard.ts: Hook that listens for paste events to capture images from clipboard and manages clipboard permission errors.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Comprehensive Internet Identity authentication hook and provider for ICP, managing login/logout flow, identity persistence, and authentication status with AuthClient integration.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Defines React Query hooks for fetching and mutating analyses and user settings, including useGetAnalyses, useAnalyzeChart, useGetSettings, and useUpdateSettings.
frontend/src/hooks/useSettings.ts: Provides a useSettings hook that wraps useGetSettings and useUpdateSettings to simplify settings management with loading state and update function.
frontend/src/index.css: Defines Tailwind CSS configuration with custom CSS variables for theming (light/dark modes), base styles for typography and form elements, and utility classes.
frontend/src/main.tsx: Application entry point that sets up React root with QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider wrappers around the main App component.
frontend/src/pages/AnalyzeChart.tsx: Page component for uploading or capturing chart images via file upload, camera, or clipboard, then navigating to processing screen with the selected image.
frontend/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx: Main dashboard page displaying app branding, AI status indicator, and navigation buttons to analyze charts, view history, and access settings.
frontend/src/pages/History.tsx: Displays a list of previous chart analyses fetched via useGetAnalyses hook, with empty state prompting users to analyze their first chart.
frontend/src/pages/ProcessingScreen.tsx: Shows animated processing stages while simulating AI chart analysis, then saves mock results and navigates to the results page.
frontend/src/pages/Results.tsx: Displays detailed analysis results including trading signals, confidence levels, chart overlay, trend direction, patterns, and support/resistance levels with share/save actions.
frontend/src/pages/Settings.tsx: Settings page component with theme toggle (dark/light), AI sensitivity slider (0-100%), default timeframe selector (M1/M5/M10), and signal notifications switch. Includes navigation back to dashboard and uses custom hooks for settings management.
frontend/src/utils/formatTimestamp.ts: Utility function that converts bigint nanosecond timestamps to formatted Portuguese date strings (e.g., '15 de Jan de 2024 às 14:30').
frontend/src/utils/shareAnalysis.ts: Utility function to share or copy analysis results (signal, confidence, trend, strength) using native Web Share API with fallback to clipboard copy for unsupported browsers.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration with custom OKLCH color system for theming, extended spacing/shadows, accordion animations, and plugins for typography, container queries, and animations.
project_state.json: Project metadata defining Advance Signal Pro as a mobile-first AI-powered chart analysis app for binary options trading, supporting M1/M5/M10 timeframes with <3s analysis target, Portuguese interface, and professional trading UI with dark/light themes.
